title: Minimum Time to Rot All Oranges
description:
Given In a grid, 0 is empty, 1 is fresh orange, 2 is rotten. Every minute, a rotten orange rots adjacent fresh ones. Return the minimum minutes to rot all oranges, or -1 if impossible.

Constraints:
- 1 ≤ m, n ≤ 200
explanation:
Intuition
Multi-source BFS seeded with all rotten oranges at time 0. Track the last time a cell becomes rotten. If any fresh remains, return -1.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Enqueue all 2's and count 1's
2. Main Algorithm: BFS level by level rotting neighbors; decrement fresh count
3. Return Result: Return minutes if fresh hits 0 else -1

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(mn)
- Space Complexity: O(mn)

Key Insights
- Multi-source BFS is a common pattern
- Track minutes by level size or time stamps

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to handle no fresh case returns wrong time
- Allowing diagonals would be incorrect
